title: SQL: When NULL is in a column,it shouldn't add primary key
poc: alter table A add constraint test1 primary key (test);
alter table A add constraint test1 primary key (test, id);
key_bug_features: NULL values in primary key column
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE B (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, test INT, name VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO B (test, name) VALUES (NULL, 'Test Name');
ALTER TABLE B ADD CONSTRAINT test_pk PRIMARY KEY (test);
INSERT INTO B (test, name) VALUES (1, 'Another Name');
ALTER TABLE B ADD CONSTRAINT test_pk_multi PRIMARY KEY (test, id);
